weeks.weekly_focus: A+ Core 1: hardware, boot process, and internal components

days.label: Day 1
days.session_objective: Kick off the week's topic and build the right vocabulary.
days.suggested_split: 15m recall + 75m core learning + 45m practice / lab + 15m wrap-up
days.todo: Watch the A+ course intro and the CPU / RAM / storage basics. Start a glossary for hardware terms you will reuse all month.
days.deliverable_or_checkpoint: Can explain what each main computer component does and how a system boots.
days.journal_prompt: End-of-day question: What one concept from a+ core 1: hardware, boot process, and internal components still feels fuzzy, and what will you revisit tomorrow?

days.label: Day 2
days.session_objective: Deepen concepts and connect them to the bigger system.
days.suggested_split: 15m recall + 75m core learning + 45m practice / lab + 15m wrap-up
days.todo: Study motherboard, buses, power delivery, BIOS / UEFI, and the boot path from power-on to login.
days.deliverable_or_checkpoint: Can explain what each main computer component does and how a system boots.
days.journal_prompt: End-of-day question: What one concept from a+ core 1: hardware, boot process, and internal components still feels fuzzy, and what will you revisit tomorrow?

days.label: Day 3
days.session_objective: Apply the concepts with examples, drills, or comparisons.
days.suggested_split: 15m recall + 75m core learning + 45m practice / lab + 15m wrap-up
days.todo: Cover ports, connectors, displays, and common form factors. Build a one-page hardware and connector cheat sheet.
days.deliverable_or_checkpoint: Can explain what each main computer component does and how a system boots.
days.journal_prompt: End-of-day question: What one concept from a+ core 1: hardware, boot process, and internal components still feels fuzzy, and what will you revisit tomorrow?

days.label: Day 4
days.session_objective: Use tools or labs so the topic becomes practical, not abstract.
days.suggested_split: 15m recall + 75m core learning + 45m practice / lab + 15m wrap-up
days.todo: Inspect your own machine or a borrowed machine: record CPU, RAM, storage, and firmware information using built-in tools.
days.deliverable_or_checkpoint: Can explain what each main computer component does and how a system boots.
days.journal_prompt: End-of-day question: What one concept from a+ core 1: hardware, boot process, and internal components still feels fuzzy, and what will you revisit tomorrow?

days.label: Day 5
days.session_objective: Synthesize the week and create the deliverable.
days.suggested_split: 15m recall + 75m core learning + 45m practice / lab + 15m wrap-up
days.todo: Teach the boot process back in plain language and close gaps with a short self-test or flashcard drill.
days.deliverable_or_checkpoint: Hardware glossary + power-on-to-login diagram
days.journal_prompt: Summarize this week in 5-8 sentences and make sure the deliverable is saved somewhere easy to find.

days.label: Day 6
days.session_objective: Lock in the week's material, surface weak spots, and tidy your notes.
days.suggested_split: 45m recall + 60m quiz / weak-topic review + 30m redo one lab + 15m next-week setup
days.todo: Recall this week from memory, quiz yourself on the weak spots, redo the most useful exercise, and clean up your notes for a+ core 1: hardware, boot process, and internal components.
days.deliverable_or_checkpoint: Weekly recap complete; one weak topic revisited; notes cleaned
days.journal_prompt: Add a short journal entry: What improved this week? What still needs another pass before moving on? Checkpoint target: Can explain what each main computer component does and how a system boots.

days.label: Day 7
days.session_objective: Rest and let consolidation happen.
days.suggested_split: Off
days.todo: No course work. Protect the day. Only do admin cleanup if it genuinely reduces stress next week.
days.deliverable_or_checkpoint: Recovery complete
days.journal_prompt: Optional: note any life constraints that may affect the next week, then close the workbook.
